w:r.w:t: Final exam: Saturday, May 9, 2026 – project presentation
Provide a concise description of the web application, its purpose, and the problem it solves.
Replace this section with the project's mission statement and key objectives.
Describe the scope of your project. You may enumerate the following
Functions or the features of your web application.
If your project is a web, desktop, or mobile application or an integration of different applications.
Specify here your general and specific objectives (3-5 objectives only).

This section describes the overall technical composition of your project. Put an introductory paragraph here before putting anything in its sub-sections. Your introduction describes the content of this chapter or section.
Insert architecture diagram here (e.g., exported PNG from draw.io or Miro).
Diagram placeholder: Client → CDN / Load Balancer → API Gateway → Backend Services → Database. Replace with actual architecture diagram.
Make sure to add a paragraph explaining your diagram.
For the design of the customer user interface.
All requests and responses use Content-Type: application/
. Dates are ISO 8601 (UTC). Pagination uses cursor-based paging.
All protected endpoints require a Bearer token in the Authorization header:
Tokens expire after 60 minutes. Use the /auth/refresh endpoint to obtain a new token silently.
Request body failed validation. See errors[] for details.
Too many requests. Retry after X-
Document each endpoint group below. Duplicate this block for each resource.
Exchange refresh token for new access token
Retrieve a single resource by ID
Retrieve a single resource by ID
All JSON field types follow JSON Schema vocabulary (string, number,
Duplicate and fill in this model block for each domain entity in your application.
JWTs signed with RS256; short-lived access tokens (60 min), long-lived refresh tokens (7 days, rotated on use).
Role-based access control (RBAC) enforced at the API layer, not only in the UI.
TLS 1.2+ enforced on all endpoints; HTTP redirects to HTTPS at the load balancer.
CORS: allowed origins whitelist configured per environment.
Sensitive fields (passwords, tokens) never logged or returned in API responses.
server-side (input validation is not a frontend concern alone).
Updated the chapter 2 adding the details of the architectural diagram
Primary areas may include one or more of the following:
The total of each row should be 100%.
April 20, 2026 submit a draft of the documentation
At least one consultation per group between April 20 – 24, 2026 onsite or online.
If April 20, 2026 – required to be onsite
